title: dianthus serratus subsp. sallentii
intro: Dianthus serratus subsp. sallentii, also known as Sallent pink or pink-fringed pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is known for its pink or white flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. In herbal preparations, Dianthus serratus subsp. sallentii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
